formations_blocks: France controlling early phases with patient possession/settling play
Senegal starting more energetically and pressing/attacking with direct forward runs
key_matchups: Kounde vs the ball over his shoulder / Senegal's early left-sided attack
Hernandez vs Sarr on Senegal's cross from the left
Upamecano vs Sarr on the early run in behind
phases.summary: Senegal begin the brighter side, using energetic direct attacks down their left and testing France immediately with crosses and runs in behind. France look to settle into possession more slowly, though the early exchanges show some discomfort at the back before Dembele starts to locate Mbappe with balls into space.

formations_blocks: Senegal jumping as one when triggered
adjustments: Senegal looking to spring quickly after turnovers
France using Kounde high on the right
key_matchups: Mane vs tracking Kounde on the right
Olise and Dembele attacking down the flank against Senegal's block
phases.summary: France begin to take control of the game, with Senegal settling into a compact 4-4-2 and pressing collectively on triggers. Senegal’s main threat is immediate transition after regains, repeatedly trying to spring forward quickly, while France look especially dangerous through high full-back positioning and direct attacks into wide or half-space channels. The clearest individual duel is Senegal’s forward outlet Jackson exploiting space against Upamecano, nearly scoring after a sharp inside-left run and shot off the post.

adjustments: France improved after half-time and 'sorted their lives out'
Senegal remained competitive and 'aren't out of this'
phases.summary: The half-time note frames a game that was level at the break, with France needing a reset after a flat first half. After the interval they looked sharper and immediately created a major chance, but Senegal stayed in the contest thanks in part to Mendy denying Mbappe again.

formations_blocks: Senegal kicking long/deep at restart, suggesting a direct or deeper restart setup
France sustaining sustained attacking pressure/high possession phase
Senegal defending in a deeper block for much of the half, with France probing through wide areas and central combinations
adjustments: Dembele increasingly popping up on the right and delivering crosses
France looking to find more between-the-lines connection, with commentary noting the absence of a Griezmann-like central attacking organiser
Upamecano stepping into long distribution to switch play forward
France progressing via wing pressure and direct dribbling after the break
Senegal’s attack briefly opens up from a turnover-and-through-ball sequence rather than sustained build-up
key_matchups: Sarr running in behind against Saliba
phases.summary: France dominated the phase territorially and sustained pressure after the interval, with Dembele increasingly influential on the right and France also trying to bypass midfield with long passes. Senegal spent long periods without an attack, but a couple of transition moments briefly exposed France before Kounde’s press led to the decisive opening and Mbappe’s 66th-minute goal.

formations_blocks: France operating with a freer attacking structure after Olise's role changed the game
Senegal pushing on and trying to find openings, but leaving space through the center
France becoming more open in midfield as the phase develops
adjustments: Olise given a freer role in behind, changing France's attack
Deschamps introduces Barcola for Dembélé, likely shifting Barcola to the left, Doué right, and Olise in behind
Senegal make attacking changes: Diarra and Mbaye on for Saar and Camara, then Ndiaye and Dieng for Gueye and Jackson, and Ciss for Gueye
France speed up their play and get Olise more involved
Senegal chase a late goal after going 2-0 down
key_matchups: Diarra vs Mbappé on the 78' foul 40 yards from goal
Diouf's crossing toward Jackson before the offside finish
Barcola creating from the left and cutting back for Olise and Mbappé
phases.summary: France take control of the phase by finding a way to get Olise on the ball in a freer role, which opens up their attack and leads to a quick 2-0 cushion through Barcola. Senegal respond with multiple substitutions and late pressure, but France's faster circulation and space through the middle keep them dangerous, culminating in Mbappé's late strike after Senegal briefly pull one back.
